title: Author filter uses substring (LIKE %..%) rather than exact match
evidence: app.py:145 SELECT * FROM books WHERE author LIKE '%'+author_filter+'%'
suggestion: Acceptable interpretation of ?author=; note it is substring matching, confirmed by test_list_books_filter_by_author expecting 'Author A' to match 'Author A Again'

title: ~8% of lines uncovered — file-based DB branch and __main__ not exercised
evidence: scores.json test_coverage=0.92; tests set DATABASE=':memory:' so the file-backed get_db/init_db/close_db branches (app.py:35-38,68-81) and the __main__ runner (app.py:241-242) are never run
suggestion: Add a test that exercises the file-backed persistence path, or accept as intentional test-only in-memory isolation

title: Author filter interpolates the query param into the LIKE pattern via string concatenation
evidence: app.py:146 ('%' + author_filter + '%',) — value is still passed as a bound parameter so it is not SQL injection, but LIKE wildcards (%,_) in user input are treated literally-as-wildcards
suggestion: Optional: escape %/_ in author_filter if literal matching is desired; not a security defect since the value is parameterized
